revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
1 revision imported: importing redirects I created with chatGPT

revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- EDIT TEMPLATE DOCUMENTATION BELOW THIS LINE -->
This template links to [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Dates and numbers]] and works in the same fashion as {{tl|citation needed}}.
==Template data== <templatedata> { "params": { "date": { "label": "Date", "type": "date", "description": "The month and year the tag was added; e.g., 'January 2013', but not 'jan13'", "autovalue": "May 2025", "example": "January 2013", "required": true }, "reason": { "description": "Replaces the default hover text.", "type": "string", "default": "This term requires quantification." } }, "format": "inline", "description": "This template should be placed after statements that need quantification." } </templatedata>
==Redirects== *{{Tl|How many}} *{{Tl|Howmany}} *{{Tl|How much}}
==See also== *[[Wikipedia:Citing sources]] *{{tl|Cite check}}, popping up a box saying an article or section may have inappropriate or misinterpreted citations. *{{tl|Clarify}}, requesting clarification of a hard-to-understand phrase. *{{tl|More citations needed}}, popping up a box saying an article or section has not been verified and may not be reliable. *{{tl|Original research inline}}, to label a passage of text that may be based upon [[Wikipedia:No original research|original research]]. *{{tl|Page needed}}, to request a missing page number to an existing reference. *{{tl|POV statement}}, to label a passage of text whose [[Wikipedia:Neutral point of view|neutrality]] is disputed. *{{tl|Quote without source}}, tagging a request for citation, used for quotations that needs citations to make it complete, but not for seemingly doubtful or false texts. *{{tl|Request quotation}}, tagging a request for quoting inaccessible source, used for requesting a direct quote from the cited source for verification. *{{tl|Unreferenced}} and {{tl|Unreferenced section}}, popping up a box saying an article or section has no citation or reference for its information. *{{tl|Unreliable source?}}, tagging a request for source verification, used for information that is doubtful or appears false. *{{tl|Verify source}}, tagging a request for source verification, used for information that is doubtful or appears false.
{{Inline cleanup tags}}
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!-- ADD CATEGORIES BELOW THIS LINE --> [[Category:Inline cleanup templates]] }}</includeonly>

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- EDIT TEMPLATE DOCUMENTATION BELOW THIS LINE -->
This template links to [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Dates and numbers]] and works in the same fashion as {{tl|citation needed}}.
==Template data== <templatedata> { "params": { "date": { "label": "Date", "type": "date", "description": "The month and year the tag was added; e.g., 'January 2013', but not 'jan13'", "autovalue": "May 2025", "example": "January 2013", "required": true }, "reason": { "description": "Replaces the default hover text.", "type": "string", "default": "This term requires quantification." } }, "format": "inline", "description": "This template should be placed after statements that need quantification." } </templatedata>
==Redirects== *{{Tl|How many}} *{{Tl|Howmany}} *{{Tl|How much}}
==See also== *[[Wikipedia:Citing sources]] *{{tl|Cite check}}, popping up a box saying an article or section may have inappropriate or misinterpreted citations. *{{tl|Clarify}}, requesting clarification of a hard-to-understand phrase. *{{tl|More citations needed}}, popping up a box saying an article or section has not been verified and may not be reliable. *{{tl|Original research inline}}, to label a passage of text that may be based upon [[Wikipedia:No original research|original research]]. *{{tl|Page needed}}, to request a missing page number to an existing reference. *{{tl|POV statement}}, to label a passage of text whose [[Wikipedia:Neutral point of view|neutrality]] is disputed. *{{tl|Quote without source}}, tagging a request for citation, used for quotations that needs citations to make it complete, but not for seemingly doubtful or false texts. *{{tl|Request quotation}}, tagging a request for quoting inaccessible source, used for requesting a direct quote from the cited source for verification. *{{tl|Unreferenced}} and {{tl|Unreferenced section}}, popping up a box saying an article or section has no citation or reference for its information. *{{tl|Unreliable source?}}, tagging a request for source verification, used for information that is doubtful or appears false. *{{tl|Verify source}}, tagging a request for source verification, used for information that is doubtful or appears false.
{{Inline cleanup tags}}
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!-- ADD CATEGORIES BELOW THIS LINE --> [[Category:Inline cleanup templates]] }}</includeonly> <noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- EDIT TEMPLATE DOCUMENTATION BELOW THIS LINE -->
This template links to [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Dates and numbers]] and works in the same fashion as {{tl|citation needed}}.
==Template data== <templatedata> { "params": { "date": { "label": "Date", "type": "date", "description": "The month and year the tag was added; e.g., 'January 2013', but not 'jan13'", "autovalue": "May 2025", "example": "January 2013", "required": true }, "reason": { "description": "Replaces the default hover text.", "type": "string", "default": "This term requires quantification." } }, "format": "inline", "description": "This template should be placed after statements that need quantification." } </templatedata>
==Redirects== *{{Tl|How many}} *{{Tl|Howmany}} *{{Tl|How much}}
==See also== *[[Wikipedia:Citing sources]] *{{tl|Cite check}}, popping up a box saying an article or section may have inappropriate or misinterpreted citations. *{{tl|Clarify}}, requesting clarification of a hard-to-understand phrase. *{{tl|More citations needed}}, popping up a box saying an article or section has not been verified and may not be reliable. *{{tl|Original research inline}}, to label a passage of text that may be based upon [[Wikipedia:No original research|original research]]. *{{tl|Page needed}}, to request a missing page number to an existing reference. *{{tl|POV statement}}, to label a passage of text whose [[Wikipedia:Neutral point of view|neutrality]] is disputed. *{{tl|Quote without source}}, tagging a request for citation, used for quotations that needs citations to make it complete, but not for seemingly doubtful or false texts. *{{tl|Request quotation}}, tagging a request for quoting inaccessible source, used for requesting a direct quote from the cited source for verification. *{{tl|Unreferenced}} and {{tl|Unreferenced section}}, popping up a box saying an article or section has no citation or reference for its information. *{{tl|Unreliable source?}}, tagging a request for source verification, used for information that is doubtful or appears false. *{{tl|Verify source}}, tagging a request for source verification, used for information that is doubtful or appears false.
{{Inline cleanup tags}}
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!-- ADD CATEGORIES BELOW THIS LINE --> [[Category:Inline cleanup templates]] }}</includeonly>

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- EDIT TEMPLATE DOCUMENTATION BELOW THIS LINE -->
This template links to [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Dates and numbers]] and works in the same fashion as {{tl|citation needed}}.
==Template data== <templatedata> { "params": { "date": { "label": "Date", "type": "date", "description": "The month and year the tag was added; e.g., 'January 2013', but not 'jan13'", "autovalue": "May 2025", "example": "January 2013", "required": true }, "reason": { "description": "Replaces the default hover text.", "type": "string", "default": "This term requires quantification." } }, "format": "inline", "description": "This template should be placed after statements that need quantification." } </templatedata>
==Redirects== *{{Tl|How many}} *{{Tl|Howmany}} *{{Tl|How much}}
==See also== *[[Wikipedia:Citing sources]] *{{tl|Cite check}}, popping up a box saying an article or section may have inappropriate or misinterpreted citations. *{{tl|Clarify}}, requesting clarification of a hard-to-understand phrase. *{{tl|More citations needed}}, popping up a box saying an article or section has not been verified and may not be reliable. *{{tl|Original research inline}}, to label a passage of text that may be based upon [[Wikipedia:No original research|original research]]. *{{tl|Page needed}}, to request a missing page number to an existing reference. *{{tl|POV statement}}, to label a passage of text whose [[Wikipedia:Neutral point of view|neutrality]] is disputed. *{{tl|Quote without source}}, tagging a request for citation, used for quotations that needs citations to make it complete, but not for seemingly doubtful or false texts. *{{tl|Request quotation}}, tagging a request for quoting inaccessible source, used for requesting a direct quote from the cited source for verification. *{{tl|Unreferenced}} and {{tl|Unreferenced section}}, popping up a box saying an article or section has no citation or reference for its information. *{{tl|Unreliable source?}}, tagging a request for source verification, used for information that is doubtful or appears false. *{{tl|Verify source}}, tagging a request for source verification, used for information that is doubtful or appears false.
{{Inline cleanup tags}}
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!-- ADD CATEGORIES BELOW THIS LINE --> [[Category:Inline cleanup templates]] }}</includeonly> [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 2 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23530060143|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- EDIT TEMPLATE DOCUMENTATION BELOW THIS LINE -->
This template links to [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Dates and numbers]] and works in the same fashion as {{tl|citation needed}}.
==Template data== <templatedata> { "params": { "date": { "label": "Date", "type": "date", "description": "The month and year the tag was added; e.g., 'January 2013', but not 'jan13'", "autovalue": "May 2025", "example": "January 2013", "required": true }, "reason": { "description": "Replaces the default hover text.", "type": "string", "default": "This term requires quantification." } }, "format": "inline", "description": "This template should be placed after statements that need quantification." } </templatedata>
==Redirects== *{{Tl|How many}} *{{Tl|Howmany}} *{{Tl|How much}}
==See also== *[[Wikipedia:Citing sources]] *{{tl|Cite check}}, popping up a box saying an article or section may have inappropriate or misinterpreted citations. *{{tl|Clarify}}, requesting clarification of a hard-to-understand phrase. *{{tl|More citations needed}}, popping up a box saying an article or section has not been verified and may not be reliable. *{{tl|Original research inline}}, to label a passage of text that may be based upon [[Wikipedia:No original research|original research]]. *{{tl|Page needed}}, to request a missing page number to an existing reference. *{{tl|POV statement}}, to label a passage of text whose [[Wikipedia:Neutral point of view|neutrality]] is disputed. *{{tl|Quote without source}}, tagging a request for citation, used for quotations that needs citations to make it complete, but not for seemingly doubtful or false texts. *{{tl|Request quotation}}, tagging a request for quoting inaccessible source, used for requesting a direct quote from the cited source for verification. *{{tl|Unreferenced}} and {{tl|Unreferenced section}}, popping up a box saying an article or section has no citation or reference for its information. *{{tl|Unreliable source?}}, tagging a request for source verification, used for information that is doubtful or appears false. *{{tl|Verify source}}, tagging a request for source verification, used for information that is doubtful or appears false.
{{Inline cleanup tags}}
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!-- ADD CATEGORIES BELOW THIS LINE --> }}</includeonly> <noinclude> [[Category:Inline cleanup templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] </noinclude>

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24957526818|time triggered pipeline]]
